design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Marketplace interference exact-identification failure by augmenting-path certificate. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are marketplace/platform experiment papers on two-sided interference and bipartite matching, plus Aronow-Samii/Savje exposure-mapping misspecification only as a distant comparator; closest banked proposals are pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1, but those use graph exposure counts rather than a matching augmenting path. Our theorem is not do-calculus, g-formula, Manski/LP bounds, or another exposure-count alias: it gives a concrete matching-geometry non-equivalence certificate. Hand object: the bipartite market path b1-s1-b2-s2. In market M_full with edges b1-s1, b2-s1, b2-s2, every maximum matching has size two and b1 is matched with probability 1. In market M_cut with edges b1-s1, b2-s1, the maximum matchings have size one; under the uniform maximum-matching tie rule b1 is matched with probability 1/2. The focal buyer b1 has the same one-hop observed seller set {s1} and the same own treatment/exposure in both markets; the difference is the length-three augmenting path b1-s1-b2-s2 that gives competitor b2 an outside option. Concrete certificate: the alternating-path indicator A_{b1,s1,b2,s2}=1 changes the focal matching functional by 1/2 while one-hop exposure summaries are identical. Kernel claim: a buyer-level exposure summary is matching-sufficient for focal match-probability effects only if it includes every alternating path from the focal buyer to each competitor outside option up to the matching-relevant radius; otherwise there is a finite pair of markets with identical focal exposure summary but different exact matching-policy estimands. Why non-trivial: the object is an augmenting-path certificate from primitive bipartite matching, not a support/overlap condition or generic graph count. Why promising: it gives a small exact obstruction to standard unit-level marketplace exposure summaries and a computable alternating-path audit. Reject if it collapses to ordinary network exposure misspecification, standard matching theory without a causal estimand, definition-unfold iff, or a toy example without a general alternating-path certificate.

proposed_from.topic: Marketplace interference exact-identification failure by augmenting-path certificate. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are marketplace/platform experiment papers on two-sided interference and bipartite matching, plus Aronow-Samii/Savje exposure-mapping misspecification only as a distant comparator; closest banked proposals are pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1, but those use graph exposure counts rather than a matching augmenting path. Our theorem is not do-calculus, g-formula, Manski/LP bounds, or another exposure-count alias: it gives a concrete matching-geometry non-equivalence certificate. Hand object: the bipartite market path b1-s1-b2-s2. In market M_full with edges b1-s1, b2-s1, b2-s2, every maximum matching has size two and b1 is matched with probability 1. In market M_cut with edges b1-s1, b2-s1, the maximum matchings have size one; under the uniform maximum-matching tie rule b1 is matched with probability 1/2. The focal buyer b1 has the same one-hop observed seller set {s1} and the same own treatment/exposure in both markets; the difference is the length-three augmenting path b1-s1-b2-s2 that gives competitor b2 an outside option. Concrete certificate: the alternating-path indicator A_{b1,s1,b2,s2}=1 changes the focal matching functional by 1/2 while one-hop exposure summaries are identical. Kernel claim: a buyer-level exposure summary is matching-sufficient for focal match-probability effects only if it includes every alternating path from the focal buyer to each competitor outside option up to the matching-relevant radius; otherwise there is a finite pair of markets with identical focal exposure summary but different exact matching-policy estimands. Why non-trivial: the object is an augmenting-path certificate from primitive bipartite matching, not a support/overlap condition or generic graph count. Why promising: it gives a small exact obstruction to standard unit-level marketplace exposure summaries and a computable alternating-path audit. Reject if it collapses to ordinary network exposure misspecification, standard matching theory without a causal estimand, definition-unfold iff, or a toy example without a general alternating-path certificate.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue and banked work is pid_network_exposure_v1, which studies partial-identification bounds under shared hidden graph completions, and pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1, which failed as a graph-count alias witness. The present proposal is exact-ID, not partial-ID: it defines focal match probability under a known maximum-matching tie rule and uses an alternating-path certificate to separate matching-policy estimands inside a buyer-summary alias class.","published_axis":"JohariLiLiskovichWeintraub2022 and MasoeroEtAl2026 establish marketplace interference and two-sided designs; AronowSamii2017 and Savje2024 establish exposure mappings and their misspecification risks; Chen2019 treats matching-market causal inference when the mechanism is simulable. None gives a finite augmenting-path summary frontier or alias-diameter certificate for exact identification of focal match probabilities under low-dimensional buyer summaries."}
seed_list: Prove the four-node augmenting-path alias theorem showing identical one-hop buyer summaries but focal match probabilities 1 and 1/2.
Characterize the alternating-path sigma-field necessary for matching-sufficient buyer exposure summaries on finite market classes.
Construct a polynomial-time augmenting-path audit that returns either zero alias diameter or a separating pair of markets.
Derive sharp partial-ID bounds for focal match effects under one-hop exposure summaries while enforcing bipartite matching feasibility.
Show that multiple-randomization marketplace designs do not identify policy-induced matching effects when the feasible matching correspondence changes outside exposure cells.
Formalize a marketplace exposure-summary alias relation and prove augmenting-path separation within an alias class.
Give a two-sided Horvitz-Thompson impossibility theorem when only summaries, not the matching mechanism, are simulable.
Extend the four-node path to an open class of markets where a competitor outside option shifts focal match probability by a bounded epsilon.
Define a corrected marketplace exposure summary adding competitor outside-option paths and prove exact identification for focal match probability on radius-one markets.
Build matching-feasible sensitivity bounds for exposure-map misspecification and compare them to unconstrained network-exposure bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prove the four-node augmenting-path alias theorem showing identical one-hop buyer summaries but focal match probabilities 1 and 1/2.

anchor_paper.author: Johari, Li, Liskovich, Weintraub
anchor_paper.claim: Marketplace interference changes participant outcomes through two-sided competition and affects design bias.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample strengthened by named marketplace design comparator and non-degenerate maximum-matching tie rule.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard matching theory because augmenting paths already govern maximum matchings.
seed_details.rebuttal: Standard matching theory does not define the causal exposure-summary alias relation or the focal exact-ID estimand.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not JohariLiLiskovichWeintraub2022 because their bias analysis lacks a finite summary-alias matching certificate.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine matching enumeration creates a causal non-equivalence certificate and exact estimand separation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Single witness might not satisfy flagship floor.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The theorem is paired with a general alternating-path frontier conjecture.

adversarial_referee.instance: Four-node example could be dismissed as a toy.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The witness has nonzero maximum-matching support and no collapsed buyer or seller arm.

adversarial_referee.instance: Marketplace papers may not claim one-hop sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft positions them as marketplace-interference anchors, not as one-hop sufficiency claims.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the alternating-path sigma-field necessary for matching-sufficient buyer exposure summaries on finite market classes.

anchor_paper.author: Savje
anchor_paper.claim: Exposure mappings should separate definitions from assumptions under misspecification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Necessary-and-sufficient risk addressed by defining sufficiency via alias diameter, not by naming the frontier tautologically.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Aronow-Samii exposure mappings because a correct exposure mapping is sufficient by definition.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture names the missing matching-path content required before a summary can be correct.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Savje2024 because it gives a matching-specific path sigma-field rather than a general warning about misspecification.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: creates a new focal object, the alternating-path sufficiency frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may be clearer than sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The v1 statement uses only the necessary direction for the flagship conjecture.

adversarial_referee.instance: Summary containing all paths may still omit other matching determinants.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The conjecture phrases the path field as necessary, not sufficient alone.

adversarial_referee.instance: Exposure mapping literature may not discuss matching markets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: That absence is the gap; marketplace anchors supply the matching setting.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a polynomial-time augmenting-path audit that returns either zero alias diameter or a separating pair of markets.

anchor_paper.author: Chen
anchor_paper.claim: Exact estimation in matching markets is available when the assignment and matching mechanism is simulable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability seed is stronger because it returns a certificate pair, not just a warning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only maximum-matching enumeration under a new name.
seed_details.rebuttal: The audit checks causal summary alias classes and returns estimand separation, which matching enumeration alone does not define.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Chen2019 because it audits insufficiency of compressed summaries before mechanism simulation.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: produces a computable evaluation map and certificate.

adversarial_referee.instance: Algorithm complexity might be promissory.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft confines the claim to finite edge-list classes and matching/flow subroutines.

adversarial_referee.instance: Audit targets match probability only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal estimand is the marketplace policy object; welfare is left outside scope.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims characterization but may only give enumeration.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The audit is secondary to the path-frontier conjecture.

seed_details.one_line: Derive sharp partial-ID bounds for focal match effects under one-hop exposure summaries while enforcing bipartite matching feasibility.

anchor_paper.author: Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp bounds for network causal effects under misspecified exposure mappings.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-ID extension is stronger only if matching feasibility changes the sharp set.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just existing misspecified-exposure bounds with a matching constraint.
seed_details.rebuttal: The matching constraint couples buyers and sellers globally and is not a unitwise exposure-propensity relaxation.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 because it imposes bipartite maximum-matching feasibility.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the matching-feasible set has a closed-form non-LP representation.

seed_details.one_line: Show that multiple-randomization marketplace designs do not identify policy-induced matching effects when the feasible matching correspondence changes outside exposure cells.

anchor_paper.author: Masoero, Vijaykumar, Richardson, McQueen, Rosen, Burdick, Bajari, Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: Multiple randomization designs address interference with interacting populations under local interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation becomes nonroutine because treatment changes feasible matching, not only cell labels.
seed_details.referee_objection: MRD papers already define direct and spillover estimands for marketplaces.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those estimands do not resolve changed feasible-matching correspondences under compressed buyer summaries.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not MasoeroEtAl2026 because the target is exact matching-policy aliasing, not MRD estimation.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if framed as non-identification under changed correspondence; otherwise field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: MRD may not promise matching-policy effects.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use MRD only as design comparator, not as a claimed false theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs a concrete estimand.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal match probability mu_b supplies it.

adversarial_referee.instance: May reuse four-node pair only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need open-class promotion; not chosen for v1.

seed_details.one_line: Formalize a marketplace exposure-summary alias relation and prove augmenting-path separation within an alias class.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: A summary must define its induced estimand and equivalence relation.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Define the count-summary equivalence relation formally

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object seed directly answers the prior failure mode by defining sigma_b aliasing and D_b.
seed_details.referee_objection: This only repairs a banked proposal's structure.
seed_details.rebuttal: The matching-policy estimand and augmenting-path separator are new relative to graph-count aliasing.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1 because it uses bipartite matching geometry, not regular graph count aliasing.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: creates a formal alias relation plus focal matching separation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Prior-proposal anchor is internal.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is cross-linked to Johari, Aronow-Samii, and Savje in the gaps payload.

adversarial_referee.instance: Alias relation alone is not enough.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The theorem computes a nonzero focal match-probability separation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could remain one finite pair.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The conjecture promotes it to a finite-class audit.

seed_details.one_line: Give a two-sided Horvitz-Thompson impossibility theorem when only summaries, not the matching mechanism, are simulable.

anchor_paper.author: Chen
anchor_paper.claim: Horvitz-Thompson style exact estimation relies on simulability of the matching mechanism.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator refutation is stronger only if tied to exact non-identification of the mechanism.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a trivial no-propensity no-HT result.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed failure is not missing propensities but summary aliasing of two simulable mechanisms.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Chen2019 because it asks when summary-level mechanism information is insufficient.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier without a new estimator or rate.

seed_details.one_line: Extend the four-node path to an open class of markets where a competitor outside option shifts focal match probability by a bounded epsilon.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Finite alias witnesses need generic class promotion.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Open-class promotion directly avoids the single-witness anti-pattern.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is still the same toy replicated many times.
seed_details.rebuttal: A bounded epsilon class would allow arbitrary nuisance edges while preserving an augmenting-path separator.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the failed graphpair proposal because the open class is defined by matching-path perturbations.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the epsilon lower bound is derived from primitive maximum-matching counts.

adversarial_referee.instance: Replication may not create an open class.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need nuisance-edge stability conditions; kept as secondary seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable map yet.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Alias diameter D_b is the candidate map.

adversarial_referee.instance: May show only one direction.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed is framed as generic sharpness, not iff.

seed_details.one_line: Define a corrected marketplace exposure summary adding competitor outside-option paths and prove exact identification for focal match probability on radius-one markets.

anchor_paper.author: Aronow, Samii
anchor_paper.claim: Exposure mappings compress assignments into received exposures for causal estimands under interference.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: mapping that relates experimental treatment assignments to exposures

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct is stronger because it names a corrected summary, not only a failure.
seed_details.referee_objection: Corrected summary is just the full local graph in disguise.
seed_details.rebuttal: The path summary is smaller than full graph on radius-one markets and targeted to mu_b.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not AronowSamii2017 because it characterizes matching-specific content required in the exposure map.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if radius-one exactness is proved without defining sufficiency tautologically.

adversarial_referee.instance: Corrected summary may be only sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Pair it with Theorem 1 necessity for omitted path.

adversarial_referee.instance: No estimator E* exists.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: For ExactID, the corrected object is the exposure summary, not an estimator.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only focal match probability.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal buyer effect is the locked marketplace estimand.

seed_details.one_line: Build matching-feasible sensitivity bounds for exposure-map misspecification and compare them to unconstrained network-exposure bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Misspecified exposure mappings can yield sharp bounds for direct and spillover effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-weakening seed is weak unless the matching constraint gives a strict non-LP frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is routine LP partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is not chosen; the exact-ID augmenting-path certificate is cleaner and less LP-like.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 supplied the map and it was reused rather than redone: Johari, Li, Liskovich, and Weintraub (2022, Management Science) anchor marketplace interference and design bias; Masoero et al. (2026, JRSSB) anchor multiple randomization designs for interacting marketplace populations; Liu, Shaikh, and Toulis (2025, arXiv) anchor randomization inference in two-sided markets; Chen (2019, SSRN) anchors causal inference in matching markets when the mechanism is simulable; Aronow and Samii (2017, AOAS) anchor exposure mappings; Savje (2024, Biometrika) separates exposure definitions from assumptions; Schroder et al. (2026, arXiv) anchor misspecified-exposure partial-ID bounds; Zigler and Papadogeorgou (2018, Statistical Science) anchor bipartite causal inference. The unresolved gap is a finite exact-ID obstruction where matching geometry, specifically an augmenting path, separates focal match probabilities despite identical one-hop buyer summaries.

{"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Pivot v1 drafts an alternating-path summary frontier for exact identification of focal marketplace match probabilities.","literature_map":"Johari, Li, Liskovich, and Weintraub (2022, Management Science) anchor marketplace competition-driven interference and design bias by market balance.
Holtz, Felipe Lobel, Lobel, Liskovich, and Aral (2024, Management Science) document marketplace interference bias in Airbnb experiments.
Masoero et al. (2026, JRSSB) formalize multiple randomization designs for interacting marketplace populations but retain local-interference estimands.
Liu, Shaikh, and Toulis (2025, arXiv) study randomization inference for two-sided market experiments rather than exact identification of matching-policy effects.
Chen (2019, SSRN) gives matching-market causal inference when the assignment and matching mechanism is simulable.
Doudchenko et al. (2020, arXiv) treat bipartite designs where treatment and outcome units differ.
Aronow and Samii (2017, AoAS) provide the exposure-mapping framework.
Savje (2024, Biometrika) separates exposure definitions from structural interference assumptions.
Schroder et al. (2026, arXiv) give sharp partial-ID bounds for misspecified network exposure mappings but not bipartite matching feasibility.
Berge (1957) and Edmonds (1965) supply augmenting-path and matching-feasibility mechanics.

Defining equation (copy from Section 6): D_{H_{b1}} = |Pr{b1 matched | M_full, mu_max} - Pr{b1 matched | M_cut, mu_max}|
RHS symbol provenance:
- B={b1,b2}, S={s1,s2}; source: Section 6 primitive
- E_full={b1s1,b2s1,b2s2}; source: Section 6 primitive
- E_cut={b1s1,b2s1}; source: Section 6 primitive
- H_{b1}(M_full)=({s1},X_b1); source: derived from E_full
- H_{b1}(M_cut)=({s1},X_b1); source: derived from E_cut
- M_max(M_full)={{b1s1,b2s2}}; source: derived by enumerating size-two matchings
- M_max(M_cut)={{b1s1},{b2s1}}; source: derived by enumerating size-one matchings
- mu_max; source: Section 7 assumed tie rule, load-bearing only for numerical value
Substituted equation: D_{H_{b1}} = |1 - (1/2)(1+0)|
Computed value: D_{H_{b1}} = 1/2
Invariant check 1: bipartite edge domain; LHS all edges in B x S; RHS required domain B x S; result = OK
Invariant check 2: maximum matching cardinality; LHS full cardinality 2 and cut cardinality 1; RHS enumerated matching sets have those cardinalities; result = OK
Invariant check 3: one-hop summary equality; LHS N_full(b1)={s1}; RHS N_cut(b1)={s1}; result = OK
Invariant check 4: path indicator separation; LHS A_{b1,s1,b2,s2}(M_full)=1; RHS A_{b1,s1,b2,s2}(M_cut)=0; result = OK
Assumption-substitution screen: mu_max is sourced from Section 7 and is load-bearing for the numeric probability 1/2, but not for the primitive existence of the AP certificate. The focal object D_{H_b1} is computed from Section 6 primitives plus the declared tie rule, not assumed as a Section 7 quantity.
SC10 verdict: PASS","SC11_proof_route_non_trivial":"Headline: Conjecture 1, AP_b is sufficient and minimally so on radius-one competitor markets
Step 0a - unfold Section 6 definitions: theta_b, D_A, H_b, AP_b, and M_1(b); not counted
Step 0b - apply Section 7 assumptions: finite market, uniform maximum matching, consistency, one-hop summary, radius-one class, summary sufficiency target; not counted
Step 1 (substantive): focal-star factorization lemma partitions maximum matchings by the focal seller chosen for b and competitor outside-option triples; counts toward k
Step 2 (substantive): outside-option cancellation lemma shows equal AP_b summaries imply equal counts of maximum matchings with b matched and unmatched; counts toward k
Step 3 (substantive): minimality embedding lemma realizes each dropped AP triple as a disjoint four-node certificate without changing retained summaries; counts toward k
Classical result: Berge alternating-path characterization of maximum matchings
k = 3; PASS iff k >= 3
Headline: Conjecture 2, positive alias diameter implies an alternating-path certificate
Step 0a - unfold Section 6 definitions: alias diameter and focal match probability; not counted
Step 0b - apply finite-market and uniform maximum-matching assumptions; not counted
Step 1 (substantive): convert unequal focal match probabilities into unequal focal edge marginals of two uniform maximum-matching distributions; counts toward k
Step 2 (substantive): couple one maximum matching from each market and decompose their symmetric difference into alternating components; counts toward k
Step 3 (substantive): prove any component changing focal matched status contains a shortest alternating path from b to a seller outside the focal summary; counts toward k
Step 4 (substantive): show that if all such path indicators were summary-measurable, the two markets could not remain in the same alias class; counts toward k
Classical result: symmetric-difference decomposition for bipartite matchings
k = 4; PASS iff k >= 3

banked_reason: Stopped early after two D-0.5 field-tier rejections across matching augmenting-path angles: angle 0 had promissory/circular AP sufficiency and audit objects, and angle 1 still promised AP frontier/certificate extraction without computing the constructive object beyond the four-node witness.
